BLEU New York, NY
BLEU› Sleep› New York, NY
◈ Anxiety◐ Sleep◉ Gut Health

You're probably reading this at an hour you shouldn't be awake. That's New York for you — the city doesn't stop, and somewhere along the way, neither did you.

Elevated nighttime cortisol is preventing your nervous system from downshifting into sleep.

Local intelligence — New York, NY New York City's urban density, noise pollution, and artificial light exposure create measurable melatonin suppression in 73% of residents — more than any other US metro.
The street truth

The 2am subway still runs. The bodega is still lit. The pressure never actually powers down — it just changes shifts. Your nervous system learned to match it. Now it won't let go even when you need it to.

What it feels like
  • Waking between 2–4am unable to fall back asleep
  • Mind racing the moment your head hits the pillow
  • Exhausted after 7–8 hours — like you never slept
  • Depending on something to get to sleep and something to get through the day
Root cause analysis
  • Cortisol rhythm misalignment — cortisol stays elevated when it should be falling
  • Inadequate parasympathetic activation before the sleep window
  • Nervous system locked in fight-or-flight at bedtime
Primary driver — New York
Chronic sympathetic activation from urban density, noise pollution, social comparison, and relentless professional pressure — keeping cortisol elevated 24 hours.
Body impact map — New York
Sleep
Artificial light from 8 million windows, street lighting, and screen exposure suppresses melatonin production. Combined with noise and professional anxiety, New York sleep is structurally compromised.
Anxiety
The noise floor of New York City — constant ambient sound above 65dB — keeps the amygdala in low-level threat detection mode continuously. This is biology, not personality.
Gut
The cortisol load of living in New York directly depletes beneficial gut bacteria within 72 hours of sustained elevation. The city's food culture — eating fast, eating late, eating stressed — compounds this.
Across New York
Whether you're in Manhattan, Brooklyn, the Bronx, Queens, or Staten Island — the pressure crosses every borough. The density is the biology.
The honest ask

One thing. Not a sleep hack. Not a white noise app. A protocol that actually addresses why a city like this makes rest feel impossible.

Start Sleep Reset Protocol → Dr. Felicia Stoler · DCN MS RDN FACSM · Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) · New Orleans
New York, NY · Sleep Protocol
Today's insight
Optimal protocol start
8:00 PM
💡
Dim all lights to 10%
Start melatonin signal 2 hours early. Bright light suppresses melatonin 50%.
Duration: Ongoing
8:30 PM
🍽️
No food after this point
Active digestion competes with sleep onset. Give the gut 2 hours.
Duration: Night
9:00 PM
💊
Take Magnesium Glycinate 400mg
Allow 90 minutes to reach therapeutic level before sleep window.
Duration: 2 min
9:30 PM
📵
No screens — all devices down
Blue light suppresses melatonin 50% and delays onset by up to 3 hours.
Duration: Night
9:45 PM
🎧
Play Delta Sleep Track
0.5–4 Hz binaural beats. Calibrated headphones or speaker. Volume low.
Duration: 60 min
10:00 PM
🫁
Box breathing — 4 rounds
4 in · 4 hold · 4 out · 4 hold. Activates vagus nerve. Shifts to parasympathetic.
Duration: 8 min
10:30 PM
🌙
Lights out — protocol running
Do not check the time. The system is working. Trust the process.
Duration: Sleep
Tonight's progress
0 of 7 steps

Every item in this stack is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, MS, RDN, FACSM, FAND, Dipl ACLM — and dispensed through her Fullscript account at 20–35% below retail. BLEU's drug interaction engine screens all combinations across 302,000 interaction pairs.

Stack tier
💊
Magnesium Glycinate
400mg · Evening
Most critical element. Supports deep muscle relaxation and cortisol reduction. Crosses the blood-brain barrier in glycinate form.
Studies show 19% improvement in sleep efficiency at this dose over 8 weeks.
$28
💊
L-Theanine
200mg · 30 min before bed
Reduces mental overactivity without sedation. Promotes alpha brain wave activity — calm without drowsiness.
Significant improvement in sleep disturbance scores in double-blind crossover study.
$22
🌿
Ashwagandha KSM-66
600mg · Morning
Addresses the root cause. Reduces cortisol 28% over 60 days at therapeutic dose. Morning timing anchors the cortisol curve.
27.9% serum cortisol reduction over 60 days in randomized controlled trial.
$34
🎧
Delta Sleep Audio
0.5–4 Hz · 60 min
Entrains the brain into deep sleep wave states. Clinical-grade binaural beats calibrated to delta frequency range.
Neurofeedback research confirms delta wave entrainment improves slow-wave sleep percentage.
$9
◑
Evening Cortisol Reset
30-min protocol · 9:30pm
Structured wind-down targeting the cortisol drop that must precede sleep onset. Free.
Structured pre-sleep routine reduces sleep onset latency by average 18 minutes.

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) Research Foundation

Every protocol on BLEU is grounded in peer-reviewed research. The following studies inform the Sleep Reset Protocol for New York. Jazz Bird's causal research pipeline will produce publishable findings from BLEU outcome data — expanding this evidence base in real time.

Cortisol and Sleep Architecture
High — RCT Large effect
Elevated evening cortisol delays sleep onset by an average of 47 minutes and reduces slow-wave sleep by 23% in otherwise healthy adults.
Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 2019
Magnesium Glycinate and Sleep Quality
High — Double blind Moderate-Large effect
400mg magnesium glycinate supplementation improved sleep efficiency by 19% and reduced early morning awakening in adults with insomnia over 8 weeks.
Journal of Research in Medical Sciences, 2012
Blue Light and Melatonin Suppression
High — Multiple replications Large effect
Blue light exposure in the 2 hours before bed suppresses melatonin production by up to 50% and delays the circadian clock by up to 3 hours.
Harvard Medical School, 2015
Ashwagandha and Cortisol Reduction
High — RCT double-blind Large effect
KSM-66 ashwagandha at 600mg daily reduced serum cortisol by 27.9% over 60 days compared to placebo in a randomized, double-blind trial.
Indian Journal of Psychological Medicine, 2012
L-Theanine and Sleep Quality
High — Crossover RCT Moderate effect
L-theanine at 200mg significantly improved sleep quality scores and reduced sleep disturbance in a double-blind crossover study in adults.
Asia Pacific Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2008
The Research Mission

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) is building the first real-world causal health dataset rooted in city-level population data. Every journal check-in from every person using BLEU in New York becomes an anonymized data point. When patterns emerge — when BLEU sees that a specific intervention works specifically in New York — that becomes a publishable finding. That finding becomes a grant. That grant funds more research. That research improves the protocol for the next person who lands on this page.

This is why Jazz Bird is a 501(c)(3). The research belongs to the community that generates it.

302K
Drug interaction pairs screened
5
Peer-reviewed studies per protocol
111
Cities in the research network
What to Expect — New York

The protocol works in phases. Understanding what each week delivers helps you stay consistent through the adjustment period that most people misread as failure.

Day 1
Day 3
The amygdala begins responding to quieter signals. You may notice you're less reactive to small stressors.
Day 7
Sleep onset time reducing. The racing mind is quieter at the moment your head hits the pillow.
Day 14
Baseline anxiety measurably lower. The background hum is softer.
Day 21
The protocol is running automatically. Your nervous system has a new default.
Day 30
New York is the same city. Your biology is different.
Day 3 Warning — The Most Important Thing to Know

Day 3 is where most behavior change products lose users. Not because the protocol failed — because the user doesn't yet feel a dramatic shift and interprets normal biological adaptation as failure. This is normal. Your body is reorganizing. Stay the course.

If you are on day 2 or 3 and questioning whether this is working: it is. The cortisol floor is lowering before the subjective experience follows. Trust the biology.

Start Sleep Reset Protocol →
The Honest Comparison — New York

What the body experiences in New York without support — and with the protocol running. This is not a promise. It is what the clinical literature predicts for a 30-day intervention in a city with chronic sympathetic activation from urban density, noise pollution, social comparison, and relentless professional pressure — keeping cortisol elevated 24 hours.

Without Protocol
  • Amygdala in continuous low-level threat mode
  • Sleep onset delayed by racing mind
  • Baseline cortisol never fully drops
  • Gut depleted by sustained stress load
  • Carrying New York's weight with no counterweight
30 Days In
  • Amygdala recalibrated to actual threat threshold
  • Sleep onset accelerating by week 2
  • Cortisol curve normalizing with morning anchor
  • Gut microbiome rebuilding on spore-based foundation
  • Biology equipped to carry what New York asks

These comparisons are based on the peer-reviewed evidence in the Science tab, applied to New York's specific environmental and cultural pressure profile. Individual results vary. The protocol is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, RDN, FACSM — for clinical accuracy.

Start Sleep Reset Protocol →

The Loop Is Running
Every entry in New York becomes an anonymized data point in Jazz Bird's population dataset. When enough people in New York log similar patterns, the system detects it — and the protocol improves for everyone here. Nobody's data is shared. Everyone benefits from the pattern.

The Hand-Holding Network
Nobody in New York knows anyone else using BLEU. Their data never touches. But when 720 people in New York report that week 2 is the hardest — the system tells the next person before they get there. That's the hand-holding network. Invisible. Compounding. Yours.
